title: Understanding Torque
text: Torque is the rotational equivalent of linear force. It is calculated as the product of the moment of inertia and the angular acceleration.

title: Moment of Inertia for a Rod
text: The moment of inertia for a rod rotating about its center is given by the formula $$I=\frac{1}{12} m l^2$$, where $$m$$ is the mass and l is the length of the rod.

title: Angular Acceleration
text: Angular acceleration can be found using the formula $$\alpha=\frac{\omega_f-\omega_i}{t}$$, where $$\omega_f$$ is the final angular velocity, $$\omega_i$$ is the initial angular velocity, and $$t$$ is the time taken.

title: Calculate Moment of Inertia
text: Calculate the moment of inertia for the propeller using the formula $$I=\frac{1}{12} m l^2$$. Remember to consider the mass and length of both blades.

title: Calculate Angular Acceleration
text: Calculate the angular acceleration using $$\alpha=\frac{\omega_f-\omega_i}{t}$$. Convert the final angular velocity from rpm to $$\frac{rad}{s}$$.

title: Calculate Torque
text: Use the formula τ=I*\alpha to calculate the torque on the propeller.
